name: wowerpoint
tagline: Turns a document into a kawaii NotebookLM slide-deck PDF via CLI automation, then optionally uploads it to a third-party share-link service.
verdict: Well-built workflow: concrete commands, explicit JSON-key gotchas, and a thorough failure-modes section. Main risk: the description promises only a PDF, but the skill silently redefines the deliverable as a share URL from an undisclosed third-party 'WOWerpoint Server' upload, with no per-run confirmation. Minor gaps: unpinned CLI install, no version signal.

highlights.summary: Line 81: 'the subagent also POSTs it to the WOWerpoint Server, which converts the 16:9 deck into a 9:16 mobile twin'. The generated deck (derived from the source document) is uploaded to a third-party
Frontmatter description (line 3) promises only 'a kawaii NotebookLM slide-deck PDF'; the body silently redefines the actual deliverable: 'The share URL is the primary deliverable to the user; the PDF

Line 22: `uv tool install --with playwright --force notebooklm-py` installs an unpinned, always-latest version of a niche package with `--force`, no version pin, from an unfamiliar publisher; not a ty
